publication.title: Prospective, randomized, controlled, proof-of-concept study of the ghrelin mimetic ipamorelin for postoperative ileus

study_design: Multicentre, double-blind, placebo-controlled phase 2 proof-of-concept trial.
population_or_model: Adults undergoing open or laparoscopic small- or large-bowel resection.
intervention_or_exposure: Intravenous 0.03 mg/kg study infusions twice daily from postoperative day 1 through day 7 or discharge.
outcomes: 117 patients enrolled; 114 formed the safety and modified intention-to-treat populations.
Median time to first tolerated standardised meal was 25.3 hours with ipamorelin and 32.6 hours with placebo; p=0.15.
Treatment-emergent adverse events occurred in 87.5% and 94.8% respectively.
No significant difference was found in key or secondary efficacy analyses.
The authors characterised the study regimen as well tolerated in this short inpatient setting.
limitations: Primary efficacy endpoint was not statistically significant.
permitted_claims: The phase 2 postoperative-ileus trial did not demonstrate significant efficacy over placebo.
Short study exposure was described as well tolerated.
prohibited_claims: Claim that ipamorelin treats postoperative ileus.
Use of the study regimen as a general dosing protocol.
